script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Morning, Sarah. June 20th already. How is it Friday again, so fast?
script.ja: おはようございます、サラさん。もう6月20日ですね。こんなに早くまた金曜日ですか？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Morning, Alex. Yes, it is. And it's quite rainy out there today. Moderate rain all day, 100% chance. High of 22, low of 20.
script.ja: おはよう、アレックス。そうね。そして今日はかなり雨が降っているわね。一日中、中程度の雨で降水確率100%。最高気温22度、最低20度よ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Ugh, really? All day? That's... not ideal for my morning commute. My umbrella is probably still in the office from last week.
script.ja: ええ、本当に？一日中ですか？それは…朝の通勤には理想的じゃないですね。私の傘、たぶんまだ会社に置きっぱなしだ。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: You'll manage. You look a bit... preoccupied this morning, though.
script.ja: どうにかなるでしょう。でも今朝は少し…浮かない顔ね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Preoccupied? Oh. Yeah, maybe. The baby was up at 3. And then 5. He just turned one year old, you know. We had a little party. My wife did most of the planning, of course. It doesn't get easier, does it? What does 'preoccupied' even mean, anyway?
script.ja: 浮かない顔？ああ。ええ、そうかもしれません。赤ちゃんが3時に起きて、それから5時にも。ちょうど1歳になったんですよ。ささやかなパーティーをしました。もちろん、妻がほとんど計画してくれましたが。楽にならないですね、やっぱり。ところで、「preoccupied」ってどういう意味ですか？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It means your mind is busy with other things. Like you're thinking about something else a lot. Mine were like that until they were two. It passes, eventually.
script.ja: 他のことで頭がいっぱいってことよ。何か他のことをたくさん考えているような状態ね。うちの子たちも2歳まではそんな感じだったわ。結局は終わるものよ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Two? Wow. I was hoping for 'it ends around one.' My coffee is barely working today. I was just reading something online about support for parents, about new government budgets for babies, and it mentioned the 'efficacy' of these programs. I paused there, wondering.
script.ja: 2歳ですか？わあ。1歳くらいで終わるかなって期待してたのに。今日のコーヒーは全然効かないな。ちょうど親向けの支援について、新しい赤ちゃんの予算についてオンラインで読んでいたんです。それで、これらのプログラムの「efficacy」について書かれていて、そこで立ち止まってしまいました。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Efficacy? It means how well something works, how effective it is. They want to know if the money actually helps families. That's a normal question.
script.ja: エフィカシー？それは、何かがどれくらいうまく機能するか、どれくらい効果的か、という意味よ。そのお金が実際に家庭を助けるかどうかを知りたいのね。それは当然の疑問だわ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: So they want to know if the programs actually achieve what they're supposed to. I guess that makes sense. I just want to achieve more sleep. Maybe a program for new dads to just... sleep. That would have high efficacy for me.
script.ja: つまり、プログラムが本来の目的を達成するかどうかを知りたいってことですね。それは理にかなっていると思います。私はただ、もっと睡眠を達成したいだけです。新しい父親がただ…眠るためのプログラムとか。それなら私にとって高い効果があるでしょうね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: I think many parents would agree. You're entering a new phase with a one-year-old. Mine started walking around then. That's when the real running began.
script.ja: 多くの親が同意するでしょうね。1歳の子どもとの新しい段階に入っていくのよ。うちの子はちょうどその頃に歩き始めたわ。そこからが本当の追いかけっこが始まったの。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Oh, great. Something to look forward to. More running. Less sleep. Anyway, this is The Daily Tanu-chan Show. And we're back talking about... new challenges.
script.ja: ああ、素晴らしい。楽しみですね。もっと走り回って、もっと睡眠時間が減る。さて、こちらはThe Daily Tanu-chan Showです。新しい課題について話しています。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Exactly. Speaking of things that bring new challenges, or at least a lot of excitement, did you know what happened on this day, June 20th, back in 1975?
script.ja: その通りね。新しい課題、あるいは少なくともたくさんの興奮をもたらすものといえば、1975年の今日、6月20日に何が起こったか知ってる？

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: 1975? No, I don't think so. Was it something important for... the economy? Or maybe a new product launch?
script.ja: 1975年ですか？いや、知らないですね。経済にとって何か重要なことですか？それとも新製品の発売とか？

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: Not quite. It was the release date for the movie 'Jaws'.
script.ja: そうじゃないわね。映画『ジョーズ』の公開日よ。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Jaws? Oh, the shark movie! Really? That old? I thought it was from the 80s, maybe. I didn't know that. My dad used to talk about how scary it was, he wouldn't go swimming in the ocean for months after seeing it.
script.ja: ジョーズ？ああ、あのサメの映画！本当ですか？そんなに古いの？80年代の映画だと思っていました。それは知らなかったな。父がよく、あれがどれだけ怖かったか話していましたよ。あれを見てから数ヶ月は海で泳がなかったそうです。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It certainly was effective, then. Many people were afraid to go in the ocean. My kids watched it when they were teenagers and thought it was funny, not scary at all. They just laughed at the special effects.
script.ja: それなら確かに効果的だったのね。多くの人が海に入るのを怖がったものよ。うちの子たちは10代の時に見て、全然怖くなくて面白いって言ってたわ。特殊効果を見て笑っていたわね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: Funny? That's a completely different reaction. Maybe it's because movies are so much more realistic now. Or maybe they just aren't scared of sharks anymore. I wonder if I'd find it scary now. Probably not as much, but I'm easily startled these days. Any sudden noise, I jump. Could be the baby.
script.ja: 面白い？それは全く違う反応ですね。たぶん今の映画の方がずっとリアルだからでしょうね。あるいは、単に彼らがサメを怖がらなくなったのかもしれません。今見たら怖いかな。たぶんそこまでじゃないでしょうが、最近は些細なことで驚いてしまいます。どんな突然の音でも飛び上がってしまう。赤ちゃんの影響かもしれないですね。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: It's the baby. It passes. Well, Alex, it sounds like you have a busy weekend ahead, with a one-year-old on the move. Maybe try to rest a little.
script.ja: 赤ちゃんね。そのうち終わるわよ。さあ、アレックス、1歳の子が動き回るから、忙しい週末になりそうね。少しは休んでみてね。

script.speaker: Alex
script.text: I will try my best. Thanks, Sarah. Good talking with you. Have a good Friday, everyone.
script.ja: 頑張ってみます。ありがとうございます、サラさん。お話しできてよかったです。皆さん、良い金曜日を。

script.speaker: Sarah
script.text: You too. See you next week.
script.ja: あなたもね。また来週。

wikipedia.text: The 1994 Imam Reza shrine bomb explosion in Iran leaves at least 25 dead and 70 to 300 injured.

parenting.title: President’s Fiscal Year 2025 Budget Is a Win for Babies
parenting.summary: Contact:  Joe Weedon (202)277-9410 jweedon@zerotothree.org WASHINGTON, DC – ZERO TO THREE, the country’s leading early childhood development nonprofit dedicated to ensuring all infants and toddlers have a strong start in life, is encouraged that President Biden’s proposed fiscal year 2025 budget includes essential and much-needed funding for programs that build a strong foundation for you
